title: podman detecting itself as "inside systemd" in a "normal" shell
body:
### Issue Description

When pulling an image in a shell, I am seeing:

```
Pulling image … inside systemd: setting pull timeout to 5m0s
```

It is not running (directly) under a systemd unit, but it *is* part of a `tmux`-generated `.scope` (to help the OOM-killer get a better handle on things in the case of runaway memory usage).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run `podman pull …` under a `systemd`-aware `tmux` in a user session
2. See timeout message

### Describe the results you received

I don't expect there to be such a timeout during "normal" usage. "inside systemd" should have better heuristics (e.g., when running directly rather than as part of some larger setup).

### Describe the results you expected

No such imposed timeline during normal usage.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Fedora 44 x86_64. User session is launched by `systemd` via units (i.e., not display manager starting a "session").

### Additional information

N/A

comments.body:
Sounds like your service is using notify and not unsetting NOTIFY_SOCKET. Podman is aware of the NOTIFY_SOCKET mechanism and using it for various things to talk to systemd because that is what the interface expects us to do.

The pull is one code path which always uses it but also see the podman run --sdnotify option which uses it by default as well unless disabled via that option.
Ah ha! That would do it. Yes, `river` does not unset it.
